query_normalized: constraining lorentz invariance violation with fermi gamma‑ray burst spectra physics
rationale: The paper provides time‑resolved GRB spectral observations with Fermi (and ASTRO‑H), thereby measuring one of the key independent variables (GRB spectra) in the domain of interest; it serves as an empirical baseline for studies that later use such spectra to constrain Lorentz‑invariance violation (acceptance category (b) / (c)).
